definition: Explicit scoring interactions (synergies and gates) between ICT concepts. Synergies produce super-additive score bonuses when concepts co-occur. Gates enforce prerequisite relationships that zero-out scores when violated.
depends_on: order_blocks (synergy: OB+FVG stack, gate: displacement required)
fair_value_gaps (synergy: FVG+OB overlap, gate: premium/discount zone)
liquidity_sweep (synergy: sweep+SMT, gate: kill zone timing)
optimal_trade_entry (synergy: OTE inside PD array stack)
volume_profile (synergy: HVN at OB, gate: LVN weakens OB)
feeds_into: entry_models (synergy scores feed into total ICT score)
conflict_resolution (gates are the constraint counterpart to synergies)
_purpose: Explicit scoring interactions between concepts. When two concepts appear together, their combined effect is MORE THAN additive. When they conflict, one gates the other.

synergies.effect: Highest-probability entry zone. Both institutional ordering AND delivery imbalance at same price. +10 to ICT score.
synergies.why: OB = where orders were placed. FVG = where delivery was imbalanced. Together = institutional conviction zone.

synergies.effect: Confirms sweep is manipulation, not breakout. +8 to reversal confidence.
synergies.why: One instrument sweeps but correlated one doesn't = the sweep is engineered. Highest-conviction reversal signal.

synergies.combination: OTE zone inside OB/FVG stack (PD array stacking)
synergies.effect: Maximum precision entry. OTE refines the exact price within the OB/FVG overlap. +5 to score.
synergies.why: Three independent concepts converge at one price = institutional consensus.

synergies.effect: Highest-probability trading window. +6 to session score.
synergies.why: Kill zone provides liquidity. Distribution phase provides direction. Together = ideal conditions.

synergies.effect: CISD at the exact manipulation→distribution transition = earliest confirmed reversal. +5 if CISD fires during distribution phase.
synergies.why: CISD marks the candle-level phase change. PO3 marks the session-level phase change. Both firing together = double confirmation.

synergies.combination: Session level sweep + order pairing target
synergies.effect: When PDL is swept, order pairing immediately identifies PDH as the draw. TP = PDH. This gives the clearest TP targeting.
synergies.why: Order pairing turns a defensive event (stop hit) into an offensive target (where price is going next).

synergies.combination: Volume profile HVN at OB zone
synergies.effect: Confirms institutional activity at the OB. Price defended this level with volume. +5 to OB quality score.
synergies.why: OB without volume support could be coincidental. OB at HVN = proven institutional defense.

synergies.combination: Fibonacci extension target at session level
synergies.effect: When a 1.618 extension aligns with PWH/PWL or PMH/PML = very high-probability TP target.
synergies.why: Mathematical projection confirms institutional targeting. Two independent methods pointing to same price.

gates.rule: OTE score should only count if OTE is in the correct premium/discount zone. OTE for longs in premium = no OTE bonus.
Order blocks only score if preceded by genuine displacement. OB without displacement = 0 OB score.
Session score only applies if the current kill zone matches the PO3 phase. Kill zone during accumulation = session score halved.
Trades against established daily bias should be auto-downgraded by 1 grade level. Bias is determined pre-session and does NOT flip mid-day.

gates.gate: Liquidity void between entry and TP
gates.rule: If a liquidity_void exists between entry and TP, expect price to accelerate through it (good — faster TP hit). But if a BPR or HVN sits between entry and TP, expect price to stall there (bad — may not reach TP).
